mentions.note: Hit a new high of 323 before easing to 317; carriers will absorb price increases and the OpenAI suit may cut near-term AI-hardware risk — own it, don't trade it.
Acquisition-driven digital-brand turnaround roll-up with strong numbers but heavy rising debt and super-voting founders; start small and leave room to buy lower.
Often poorly stocked at Cramer's local store, making it the weaker of the dollar-store pair.
Preferred dollar store — well-stocked with strong seasonal value; a natural winner when consumers trade down.
Persian Gulf competitors going offline lifts industry prices to pure profit for Dow, which also uses cheaper domestic energy.
Too many translation tools now available; no need to learn a language, so Cramer passes.
Deeply undervalued even after its secondary; a Waymo spin/float could unlock a large re-rating given its self-driving lead over Tesla.
Cramer's favorite bank reports tomorrow but would need to vastly exceed consensus just to add ~4%; banks lack tech's upside.
Speculative buy on defense momentum including a Golden Dome award and Stryker vehicle systems.
Confirmed plan to monetize excess compute capacity, delivering nearly 100 points this month; exemplifies big tech's ability to create its own catalysts.
Gulf disruption makes Mosaic an instant low-cost fertilizer producer as commodity prices rise.
Top-performing refiner positioned to raise prices as oil jumps on Iran/Gulf risk.
Beneficiary of a memory shortage that may persist until 2028, driving major fortunes across memory, storage, and semicap names.
Won't move higher unless it dramatically expands and more aggressively runs its buyback; stock manipulated down into the close near 203-204 daily.
Apple trade-secret suit plus the NYT copyright case and dozens of IP claims form a serious, non-dismissable overhang that clouds IPO prospects.
Once-consistent grower delivered a weak quarter and severely punished holders; lacks control over its own destiny versus tech.
Caller-highlighted; Cramer agrees it can be bought here given large profits, strong free cash flow, and little debt.
Among the best refiner performers to own into an oil-spike environment.
Diversified global bank up 200%+ since 2025 with best-in-class cost efficiency; Webster deal builds a ~$300B US bank and a path to 18%+ ROTE by 2028.
$14.5B Element Solutions deal is strategically brilliant (materials colossus for semis/nuclear) but both stocks sold off; a good deal lost in the shuffle for now.
Chapel Trust holding; excess-merchandise trade could deliver a quick 10-point move as oil recovers.
Favorite pure-play refiner; can raise pump prices immediately, export gasoline for more profit, and benefits from limited refining capacity.
Great commercial bank being acquired by Santander US; regulatory approval secured, complementary consumer/commercial fit with strong funding via HSA business.
Rolling back prices in a way that should bring shoppers back despite a soft quarter; a reliable bounce-back name.
